channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE announces withdrawal from OPEC, analysts warn of quota chaos
item.description: The UAE has announced it is pulling out of OPEC, a move that analysts say could complicate the group's ability to manage production quotas and coordinate global oil policy.

item.title: 🇵🇭🇺🇸🎯 US-Philippines deploy anti-ship missiles near Taiwan in joint war games
item.description: Philippine and US forces showcased the NMESIS anti-ship missile system in Batanes province during annual exercises, as tensions simmer over Taiwan and the South China Sea.

item.title: ⚖️🇮🇷 Nobel laureate Mohammadi hospitalized in Iran, Nobel committee urges release
item.description: Jailed Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi was urgently hospitalized after a cardiac crisis. The Nobel Committee chief warned her life is in Iran's hands and called for her release.

item.title: 🇩🇪🚗💰 Trump auto tariffs could cost Germany nearly $18 billion in output
item.description: President Trump's tariff hike on cars and trucks from the EU could cost Germany nearly 15 billion euros in output, according to a German economic institute.

item.title: ⚔️🇺🇦 Russia claims capture of Myropillia village, Ukraine denies
item.description: Russia's Defence Ministry said its troops took control of Myropillia in Ukraine's Sumy region, but Ukraine's military denied the village had changed hands.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Offers Strait of Hormuz Deal as Trump Rejects Peace Proposal
item.description: Iran presented a proposal to mediate the Strait of Hormuz dispute through Pakistan, but US President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with the offer. Fighting could resume after four weeks of suspended bombing.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian Forces Close In on Ukrainian Stronghold Kostiantynivka
item.description: Russian troops are advancing toward Kostiantynivka in eastern Donetsk, attempting to establish a foothold near one of Ukraine's most heavily defended positions, according to Kyiv's top military commander.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Two Killed in Russian Attack on Bus in Kherson
item.description: A Russian attack on a bus in Ukraine's Kherson region killed two people and wounded several others, local officials reported.

item.title: 🛢️ Iraq Says Oil Output Can Recover Within Week Once Hormuz Crisis Ends
item.description: Iraq's oil minister stated that the country's oil production and exports can return to normal levels within one week once the Strait of Hormuz crisis is resolved.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Standoff Could Leave Trump Worse Off Than Before War
item.description: The US-Iran conflict has failed to deliver a decisive military or diplomatic victory, with analysts warning that Trump may emerge from the standoff in a weaker position than before the war began.

item.title: 🌍 Mali in Crisis: Jihadist Fighters and Tuareg Separatists Threaten Bamako
item.description: Jihadist fighters and Tuareg separatist groups are advancing toward Mali's capital Bamako, threatening the government as the security situation deteriorates across the country.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea's Hanwha Builds Arms Empire as Ukraine-Iran Wars Spur Spending
item.description: South Korea's Hanwha Group is rapidly expanding its defense business, capitalizing on increased global military spending driven by the wars in Ukraine and Iran.

item.title: 🇵🇪 Peru Launches Investigation Into Citizens Trafficked to Fight for Russia
item.description: Peru's government has opened an investigation into reports that Peruvian citizens were trafficked to fight in Russia's war against Ukraine.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Warns Shipping Firms About Paying Transit Fees Through Hormuz
item.description: The US is warning shipping companies about potential sanctions for paying Iran to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, complicating the maritime standoff.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Blocks US Sanctions Against Five Refineries
item.description: China's Commerce Ministry has blocked US sanctions against five refineries, further straining economic relations between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan's Taiyo Oil to Receive Russian Cargo as Iran War Limits Gulf Supply
item.description: Japanese refinery Taiyo Oil will receive crude from Russia's Sakhalin-2 project as Japan seeks alternative petroleum sources amid the US-Iran conflict constraining Gulf supplies.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Air Traffic Returns to Normal After Precautionary Measures Lifted
item.description: The UAE announced that air traffic has returned to normal after precautionary measures implemented during regional tensions were lifted.

item.title: ⛪ Israel Condemns Assault on Catholic Nun at David's Tomb in Jerusalem
item.description: Israel condemned an assault on a Catholic nun at David's Tomb in Jerusalem, amid rising religious tensions in the city.

item.title: 🔋 China's EV Battery Makers Widen Lead to Over 70% Global Share
item.description: Chinese EV battery manufacturers have expanded their dominance, now controlling more than 70% of global market share as competitors struggle to keep pace.

item.title: 🇪🇺 UK and European Naval Force to Counter Russia in Arctic Waters
item.description: The UK and European nations are forming a joint naval force to counter Russian presence in Arctic waters as geopolitical competition intensifies in the region.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine and Norway to Launch Joint Production of Mid-Strike Drones
item.description: Ukraine and Norway announced plans to jointly produce mid-range strike drones, strengthening Kyiv's domestic weapons manufacturing capabilities.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Denounces Japan and EU Over South China Sea Remarks at UN
item.description: China's foreign minister denounced Japan and the European Union at the United Nations over their statements on the South China Sea dispute.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Top Republicans Express Concern Over Trump's Germany Troop Withdrawal
item.description: Senior Republican lawmakers have voiced concerns about President Trump's plan to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, warning it could weaken NATO and embolden Russia.

item.title: 🌍 BBC: Inside Chornobyl - A Reporter's Journey
item.description: A BBC reporter documents their journey inside the Chornobyl exclusion zone, capturing the aftermath of occupation and radiation risks.

item.title: 🌍 BBC: The Battle for Ukraine's Eastern Front
item.description: BBC analysis of the intensifying battle for Ukraine's eastern front as Russian forces press their advantage in the Donetsk region.
